title: InstCombine: extend select-equiv to support vectors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests wide vector types (<16 x i8>) beyond the narrow vectors (<2 x>, <4 x>) in existing tests. Also uses splat constant patterns in both comparison and arithmetic operations, which tests constant folding through wider vector lanes.

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define <16 x i8> @wide_vector_splat_constant_equivalence(<16 x i8> %x, <16 x i8> %y) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <16 x i8> %x, <i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7>
  %sel = select <16 x i1> %cmp, <16 x i8> <i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17, i8 17>, <16 x i8> %y
  ret <16 x i8> %sel
}

tests.source_program:
define <4 x i32> @vector_select_with_safe_intrinsic(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %x, <i32 5, i32 5, i32 5, i32 5>
  %abs = call <4 x i32> @llvm.abs.v4i32(<4 x i32> %x, i1 false)
  %sel = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> %abs, <4 x i32> %y
  ret <4 x i32> %sel
}

declare <4 x i32> @llvm.abs.v4i32(<4 x i32>, i1)
tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests the intrinsic path in isNotCrossLaneOperation which allows trivially vectorizable intrinsics. The summary mentions safety checks for cross-lane operations but doesn't explicitly cover the intrinsic allowance path. This verifies that safe intrinsics don't block the optimization.

alive2.src:
define <4 x i32> @vector_select_with_safe_intrinsic(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %x, <i32 5, i32 5, i32 5, i32 5>
  %abs = call <4 x i32> @llvm.abs.v4i32(<4 x i32> %x, i1 false)
  %sel = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> %abs, <4 x i32> %y
  ret <4 x i32> %sel
}

declare <4 x i32> @llvm.abs.v4i32(<4 x i32>, i1)
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define <4 x i32> @vector_select_with_safe_intrinsic(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %x, { 5, 5, 5, 5 }
  %abs = abs <4 x i32> %x, 0
  %sel = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> %abs, <4 x i32> %y
  ret <4 x i32> %sel
}
=>
define <4 x i32> @vector_select_with_safe_intrinsic(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %x, { 5, 5, 5, 5 }
  %sel = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> { 5, 5, 5, 5 }, <4 x i32> %y
  ret <4 x i32> %sel
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests identity shuffle (lane-preserving, not cross-lane) with ugt comparison - demonstrates the safety check allows lane-preserving shuffles while blocking cross-lane ones, pattern not in existing tests

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define <4 x i8> @vector_and_ugt_with_lanesafe_shuffle(<4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %y, <4 x i8> %z) {
  %cmp = icmp ugt <4 x i8> %x, <i8 50, i8 50, i8 50, i8 50>
  %result = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %y
  ret <4 x i8> %result
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define <4 x i8> @vector_and_ugt_with_lanesafe_shuffle(<4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %y, <4 x i8> %z) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp ugt <4 x i8> %x, { 50, 50, 50, 50 }
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %z, 0, 1, 2, 3
  %result = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i8> %shuffle, <4 x i8> %y
  ret <4 x i8> %result
}
=>
define <4 x i8> @vector_and_ugt_with_lanesafe_shuffle(<4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %y, <4 x i8> %z) {
#0:

  %result = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %y
  ret <4 x i8> %result
